<!--
Thanks for contributing to Imagined Cosmos!

This project reproduces published Running Vacuum Model physics — keep claims
faithful to the cited papers and reference public issues only. See
CONTRIBUTING.md for the workflow and the DCO sign-off requirement, and
SECURITY.md for how to report vulnerabilities (do NOT use a PR for those).
-->

## Summary

What does this PR do, and why?

## Related issue

Closes #<!-- issue number -->

## Type of change

- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature / enhancement (non-breaking)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that changes existing behavior)
- [ ] Physics / numerical change (affects computed results — see below)
- [ ] Docs / whitepaper only
- [ ] Build, CI, or tooling

## Physics / reproduction impact

<!-- Required if this changes any computed value. Otherwise write "N/A". -->

- Does this change any computed result or eval assertion? If so, which, and what
  is the before/after value?
- Which published result / equation / citation does the new behavior match?

## Testing done

Describe how you verified the change. At minimum, the commands you ran:

- [ ] `npm run lint`
- [ ] `npm run build`
- [ ] `npm run eval` (reproduction / Friedmann / ΛCDM-limit checks pass)

## Checklist

- [ ] I read [CONTRIBUTING.md](../CONTRIBUTING.md).
- [ ] I signed off my commits (DCO) with `git commit -s`.
- [ ] My change keeps claims faithful to the cited literature (or is non-physics).
- [ ] I ran the build and eval harness locally and they pass.
- [ ] Docs / whitepaper updated where relevant (or not needed).
- [ ] This PR references public issues only and contains no security-sensitive details.
